informal_statement.text: Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is sorted strictly ascending and an integer key, return a new BST identical to the input on every existing key plus an additional node with the given key, unless the key is already present, in which case the tree is returned unchanged.
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order traversal is strictly ascending)
required_properties: the output tree is a valid BST
the multiset of keys in the output equals the multiset of keys in the input union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the multiset of keys in the input
every key present in the input is also present in the output
edge_cases: single-node tree, key less than root
single-node tree, key greater than root
single-node tree, key equal to root (unchanged)

semantic_units.description: BST precondition: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order strictly ascending).
BST invariant preservation: the output tree is also a valid BST.
common_failure_modes: asserting it only when the key is absent

semantic_units.description: Key-set change: the output's key multiset is the input's multiset union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the input's multiset.
common_failure_modes: asserting insertion regardless of duplicates (breaks BST uniqueness)

semantic_units.description: Duplicate no-op: inserting an existing key returns a tree with the same key multiset.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → IsBst (bstInsert t k)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST, the output tree preserves the BST invariant.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → (k ∈ keys t ∨ (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (k :: keys t))
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was absent, the output's keys are the input's keys plus the new key.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → k ∈ keys t → (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (keys t)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was already present, the output's keys are a permutation of the input's.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem inorder_strictly_sorted_of_input {t : BST α} (h : InorderStrictAsc t) : InorderStrictAsc t := h
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input BST has an in-order traversal that is strictly ascending (i.e., it is a valid BST with no duplicate keys).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem mem_preservation_or_add {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} : Mem (insert t k) k ↔ True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion, the resulting tree contains the key k (either it was already present or a new node is added).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem mem_existing_keys_unchanged {t : BST ℤ} {k x : ℤ} (hx : Mem t x) (hk : x ≠ k) : Mem (insert t k) x
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Every key x already present in the input tree and different from k remains present in the output tree.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem no_new_keys_other_than_k {t : BST ℤ} {k x : ℤ} (hx : Mem (insert t k) x) : Mem t x ∨ x = k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output tree does not introduce any new keys except possibly the inserted key k.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem insert_idempotent_on_existing {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} (hk : Mem t k) : insert t k = t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If k is already present in the input tree, insertion returns the original tree unchanged.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem insert_adds_when_missing {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} (hk : ¬ Mem t k) : Mem (insert t k) k ∧ (∀ x, Mem (insert t k) x → Mem t x ∨ x = k) ∧ ¬ Mem t k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If k is not present, insertion adds k while preserving all existing keys and adding no other keys.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem inorder_sorted_of_output {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} (h : InorderStrictAsc t) : InorderStrictAsc (insert t k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output tree remains a valid BST with strictly ascending in-order traversal.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem inorder_multiset_relation {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} : Inorder (insert t k) = (if Mem t k then Inorder t else insert_sorted (Inorder t) k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The in-order sequence of the output equals the input sequence if k is already present; otherwise it equals the input sequence with k inserted into the sorted order.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem insert_matches_rust_reference {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} : insert t k = rust_insert t k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The Lean function `insert` is definitionally/semantically equivalent to the provided Rust reference implementation.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem termination_insert {t : BST ℤ} {k : ℤ} : Terminates (insert t k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive structure of the implementation terminates (e.g., recursion decreases on subtree size).
